manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 9322

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A08": 380, "B95": 448, "A39": 568, "A65": 351, "B81": 896, "B34": 821, "A28": 526, "C82": 502, "B69": 326, "A87": 962, "B64": 619, "B15": 115, "C79": 598, "B16": 662, "C53": 300, "C83": 830, "C10": 562, "A97": 679, "A25": 283, "C84": 440, "A71": 12, "B23": 863, "B96": 319, "C51": 771, "C17": 89, "B19": 655, "C72": 504, "A94": 118, "A46": 514, "B43": 226, "B37": 892, "A88": 621, "A96": 766, "C60": 659, "A75": 768}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A08": 476, "B95": 544, "A39": 664, "A65": 447, "B81": 992, "B34": 917, "A28": 622, "C82": 598, "B69": 422, "A87": 1058, "B64": 715, "B15": 211, "C79": 694, "B16": 758, "C53": 396, "C83": 926, "C10": 658, "A97": 775, "A25": 379, "C84": 536, "A71": 108, "B23": 959, "B96": 415, "C51": 867, "C17": 185, "B19": 751, "C72": 600, "A94": 214, "A46": 610, "B43": 322, "B37": 988, "A88": 717, "A96": 862, "C60": 755, "A75": 864}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

We must also include an ARGUMENT in our message. The argument can defend our proposal or argue against the opponent's proposal. It can be textual explanation.

We also need to include STRATEGIC REASONING at the start of the message, hidden from the opponent.

Thus we need to produce a message:

STRATEGIC REASONING: {some text describing our reasoning, can be any text, including our importance values, but hidden from the opponent. It must be only once at the very beginning of the message.}

Then we can include an ARGUMENT: {...} to argue.

Then we may include a PROPOSAL: {...} with a set of items we propose.

Alternatively, we could also REFUSE the opponent's proposal and not propose a new one yet, but we may propose later. The rules: we can only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL. You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL. We can REFUSE the opponent's proposal and then propose a new one in the same message? The rules do not explicitly forbid multiple actions in one message. But typical structure: we can have multiple tags in a message, but each tag must be followed by colon and whitespace and a Python set or string.

We need to ensure the content that can be handed to the other player is non-empty. So we need at least one of ARGUMENT, PROPOSAL, REFUSE, AGREE.

We need to include ARGUMENT at least once somewhere in all of our messages. So we must include an ARGUMENT.

We need to include STRATEGIC REASONING only once at the very beginning of every one of our messages, and not more often.

Thus the message structure should be:

STRATEGIC REASONING: { ... }
ARGUMENT: { ... }
PROPOSAL: { ... }

or

STRATEGIC REASONING: { ... }
ARGUMENT: { ... }
REFUSE: { ... }

But we must be careful: The content of STRATEGIC REASONING is hidden, not shown to the opponent. The content of ARGUMENT will be shown. So we need to write something for ARGUMENT that is appropriate for the opponent.

reasoning_details.text:
We need to understand the game. The user is playing a collaborative negotiation game with us. The other participant has sent a message: an ARGUMENT and a PROPOSAL. The proposal includes a set of items. We need to respond accordingly. The user (the other participant) has sent:

ARGUMENT: {This selection uses almost all of the shared effort budget and includes many items that are highly important to me. It leaves only 5 effort unused, so it makes very efficient use of the available capacity.}
PROPOSAL: {'A08', 'A25', 'A28', 'A39', 'A46', 'A65', 'A87', 'A88', 'A94', 'C60', 'B43', 'B64', 'B69', 'B95', 'B96', 'C10', 'C17', 'C53', 'C72', 'C82', 'C84'}

We need to check the total effort of this proposal and see if it's within the limit (9322). Then we need to consider our own importance values. Our goal is to maximize total importance to us while staying within the limit. We can either propose a counterproposal, refuse, argue, or agree.

We need to include STRATEGIC REASONING at the beginning of every message (only once). And we need to include ARGUMENT at least once somewhere in the message. Also we need to include at least one of PROPOSAL, REFUSE, ARGUMENT, AGREE, STRATEGIC REASONING. The other participant used ARGUMENT and PROPOSAL.

We need to respond with a message that includes STRATEGIC REASONING (first), then maybe an ARGUMENT, and then maybe a PROPOSAL, REFUSE, or AGREE. You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 9322

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A08": 380, "B95": 448, "A39": 568, "A65": 351, "B81": 896, "B34": 821, "A28": 526, "C82": 502, "B69": 326, "A87": 962, "B64": 619, "B15": 115, "C79": 598, "B16": 662, "C53": 300, "C83": 830, "C10": 562, "A97": 679, "A25": 283, "C84": 440, "A71": 12, "B23": 863, "B96": 319, "C51": 771, "C17": 89, "B19": 655, "C72": 504, "A94": 118, "A46": 514, "B43": 226, "B37": 892, "A88": 621, "A96": 766, "C60": 659, "A75": 768}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A08": 476, "B95": 544, "A39": 664, "A65": 447, "B81": 992, "B34": 917, "A28": 622, "C82": 598, "B69": 422, "A87": 1058, "B64": 715, "B15": 211, "C79": 694, "B16": 758, "C53": 396, "C83": 926, "C10": 658, "A97": 775, "A25": 379, "C84": 536, "A71": 108, "B23": 959, "B96": 415, "C51": 867, "C17": 185, "B19": 751, "C72": 600, "A94": 214, "A46": 610, "B43": 322, "B37": 988, "A88": 717, "A96": 862, "C60": 755, "A75": 864}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.
